blocks.content: The salt air hit before the door was all the way open. Different from the city — older, and clean.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: This is way nicer than the photos.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Living room's bigger, too. I was expecting narrower.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: The reviews were all five stars and I still kept second-guessing it. The photos had weird angles — I was convinced they were hiding something.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Owner stays here part of the year. Floors are too good for a rental.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I was thinking the same thing. And that couch — Julian, look at that couch.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Go sit on it.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I can tell just from here. Bad rental couch ruins the whole trip.
Julian dropped both bags and crossed the room. Sat down. Said nothing for a second.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Okay. You were right.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: From the doorway.
She sat down next to him.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I could do the whole weekend from this couch and call it a success.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: There's a grill on the deck. I saw it coming in.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Of course you did.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: It's a Weber. Newer one.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I genuinely cannot tell if you're excited about the grill or just excited.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Both.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Good. I'm glad that drive is over — I don't want to see a highway again until Sunday.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Cleared up past Richmond. Better than I expected.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You were asleep at Richmond.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I woke up. I saw a sign.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: And then immediately asked me about the Harmon audit.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: The timing window doesn't add up. I kept running it.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: It'll keep till Monday. I needed this — cases back to back since February and I didn't realize how tired I was until I got in the car this morning.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I didn't realize either. I should have.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Neither of us did. That's the problem with cases — you just keep going.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Come look at the back before you do anything else.
The deck ran the full width of the house. Open sand, then the Atlantic — dark blue past the sandbar, afternoon light going warm and flat.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Low tide. We've got a good stretch.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: We should walk it before the light goes.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Five minutes with the kitchen first.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You're on vacation.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I know. I spotted a fish market on the way in — two miles south. If it's worth going to, we do it in the morning. I just want to know what I'm working with.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You had a meal plan before we crossed the state line.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: It's not a plan yet. It's an idea.
Real stove, solid counter space. A cast iron someone had left behind that hadn't been cleaned right.
That was fixable.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You're already planning the whole thing, aren't you.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: I need to see what the market has first.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: So yes.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Possibly.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Come outside before you rearrange the spice rack.
Next door, six people had a speaker on the deck and a fire pit going in the yard. Someone laughing. Chairs in a loose circle.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Should we go say hi?

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Maybe at the fire tonight.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You really can't help yourself.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: No.

blocks.prompt: They're here. Three days. What does Julian actually want from this?

options.label: To actually rest. He can't remember the last time he tried.
To cook something good. That's what relaxing looks like for him.
To make Mara glad she pushed for this.
